content:
Member-only story

I only wanted to automate one annoying task in my browser. A few hundred lines of JavaScript later, I had a tool that saved me hours every week — and eventually became something other developers and businesses were willing to pay for.

5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Caspar Camille Rubin on Unsplash1. The Browser Was Doing Too Much… and I Was Doing Too Little About It
Every morning started the same way.
Open Gmail.
Check Slack.
Download reports.
Copy analytics.
Update Notion.
Reply to repetitive emails.
It wasn’t difficult work.
It was repetitive work.
The strange part was that almost everything happened inside a browser.
I kept asking myself:
“If JavaScript already runs inside the browser, why am I still clicking the same buttons every day?”
